summary: In this paper abstract, Kaiming He and co-authors introduce the Parametric Rectified Linear Unit (PReLU) and a robust initialization method tailored for rectifier nonlinearities. These contributions enabled the training of extremely deep models from scratch and achieved the first result to surpass human-level performance on the ImageNet classification challenge.

principles.statement: Rectified activation units are essential for state-of-the-art neural networks.
principles.rationale: They are foundational components required to achieve top-tier performance in deep learning tasks like image classification.
principles.source_quote: Rectified activation units (rectifiers) are essential for state-of-the-art neural networks.

principles.statement: Initialization methods must explicitly account for rectifier nonlinearities to train extremely deep models.
principles.rationale: A robust initialization method tailored to rectifiers allows networks to be trained directly from scratch, unlocking the ability to investigate deeper and wider architectures.
principles.source_quote: Second, we derive a robust initialization method that particularly considers the rectifier nonlinearities. This method enables us to train extremely deep rectified models directly from scratch and to investigate deeper or wider network architectures.

signature_quotes.text: To our knowledge, our result is the first to surpass human-level performance (5.1%, Russakovsky et al.) on this visual recognition challenge.
signature_quotes.context: Announcing the breakthrough ImageNet classification result achieved using PReLU-nets.
